comment: Falsifiability registry: every step in VALIDATE_STEPS ∪ STOP_HOOK_STEPS must carry at least one mechanical proof that it CAN go red. Proof kinds: 'fixture' (a repo test file that spawns the real gate and asserts exit 1 — ref must exist), 'selftest' (a literal step-name string that must appear in .github/workflows/selftest.yml), 'runner' (the step wraps a vendor tool whose non-zero exit is the red; OUR wiring's failure propagation is proven by the validate-runner tests — ref must exist). scripts/check-canary-coverage.mjs enforces bidirectional closure: an unregistered step OR a stale registry entry reds the PR that caused it. Guard-rule falsifiability is now a PER-RULE closure, not a count: check-canary-coverage.mjs imports the pure-data rule tables from .claude/hooks/lib/guard-rules.mjs (BASH_RULES, WRITE_PROTECTED, WRITE_GLOBAL_CHECKS) and asserts every rule id has a behavioral deny/allow case (a RULE_CANARIES entry) in tests/hooks/hook-contract.test.mjs — a rule added without a canary reds the PR. hookRules below keeps only a human-readable grep-able deny example per surface.

format.note: biome ci exits non-zero on any diff

types.note: tsc -b exits non-zero on type errors

lint.note: eslint --max-warnings 0 exits non-zero on any finding

provenance.ref: Canary 3: strip SOURCE citations from decision sites → provenance must FAIL

provenance.note: wrong-group corpus cite on a decision site → provenance red naming site, group, and cited groups; tools/provenance-overrides.json is the reviewed escape; pre-0.1.5 baseVersion manifests downgrade to a ramp NOTE
bare-URL SOURCE on a host outside tools/lib/citation-domains.mjs → provenance red naming the host; same pre-0.1.5 ramp NOTE

C02 (v0.1.6): every corpus entry must declare `groups` (missing key = hard fail, never ramped); a presence-only (groups: []) entry can no longer wildcard-justify a flagged decision site (was: 25/35 shipped entries were universal justifiers)

G27 (v0.1.6): a consumer decision-group in tools/decision-groups.json (write-guard-protected) makes its domain constants flagged decision sites, and the coverage lockstep forces a grounding corpus entry; a malformed extension fails closed

tauri-policy.ref: Canary 1: weaken tauri.conf CSP to null → tauri-policy must FAIL

prompts.ref: Canary 7: tamper a hash-locked prompt → prompts must FAIL

schema-rls.ref: Canary 4: remove FORCE RLS from the migration → schema-rls must FAIL

migrations.ref: Canary 2: edit an existing migration → migrations must FAIL

dead-code.note: knip --strict exits non-zero on unused exports/files

architecture.note: depcruise exits non-zero on rule violations; the DAL-boundary rules were proven firing when added

build.note: gzip ratchet: a committed tools/perf-baseline.json with a tiny total → build red naming measured vs baseline × ratioCap and the `pnpm perf:baseline` re-baseline ceremony; exactly AT the cap stays green

self-disable + fail-closed: absent baseline → NOTE naming the file and `pnpm perf:baseline` while the absolute byte budgets keep their 0.1.4 behavior; malformed baseline JSON/shape → red, never fail-open

styleguide.note: primitive boundary: a hand-rolled <button className=…> outside the controlPrimitives home → red with the FIX line naming the Button/Input primitive; controlAllow (file + reason) is the reviewed escape and malformed/stale entries fail closed; a manifest without controlPrimitives self-disables with the update --refresh-seeded adoption NOTE

AAA tier: an ink between the AA and AAA boundaries (~6.2:1 on the light canvas) → red printing '(min 7:1)' — the body-text pairs (ink/canvas, ink/surface) enforce 7:1 per-pair while ink-muted/accent stay at the honest 4.5 floor, so the red can only come from the raised min

status channel (0.1.6): a .tsx announcing role=alert/role=status/aria-invalid with no danger/success token → red naming the file (the failure toast that looked exactly like a confirmation); a token NAMED only inside a comment does not count (comments are stripped — the check must never fail open); a statusSurfaces token absent from tokens[] → red (the utility would compile to nothing); statusSurfaces.allow (file + reason) is the reviewed escape and stale entries fail; a manifest without statusSurfaces self-disables with the update --refresh-seeded adoption NOTE

styleguide.ref: Canary 5: raw hex + erased palette utility in a TSX → styleguide must FAIL

perf-budget.note: dense-feature closure: a features/* dir importing useVirtualWindow/useRovingGrid with no perfSubject.ts → red with the create-FIX line naming the worked matrix pattern; exempt[] is the reviewed escape and malformed/stale exempt entries fail closed

subjects[] closure inverse: an entry pointing at a missing file and an undeclared features/*/perfSubject.ts both red before any measurement; declaring subject AND subjects together is an ambiguity red; legacy single-subject budgets keep 0.1.4 behavior plus a NOTE naming the refresh command

G30 (v0.1.6): the subject's marker count must SCALE with declared cells — a degenerate one-row perfSubject still contains role=gridcell and used to 'pass' the budget in ~1ms; it now reds naming rendered-vs-declared (markerScales:false is the reviewed opt-out for a container marker). G13 (v0.1.6): density is a SHAPE, not two import names — aria-rowcount / role=grid / <canvas> in a features/* dir with no perfSubject.ts reds, ramped to baseVersion 0.1.6 so an upgraded consumer's existing grid/canvas screen is a NOTE first; densitySignals[] in perf-budget.json is the reviewable extension point

route-manifest.ref: Canary 6: unregistered features directory → route-manifest must FAIL

e2e.ref: Canary 8: replace a route's EmptyState with a bare-word div, or its Skeleton with a silent spinner, or a route panel wider than the 640px min window, or remove the :focus-visible outline → the e2e lane (states/reflow/a11y quality checks, all iterating ROUTES) must FAIL

docs-sync.note: renaming/removing a numbered gates-catalog section reds the catalog-lockstep sub-check; a pre-0.1.5 baseVersion manifest downgrades it to a ramp NOTE

agent roster: granting a reviewer Write or Bash, dropping its disallowedTools, omitting its tools list, or unparseable frontmatter each red the roster sub-check — reviewers are read-only by construction; deliberately NOT ramped (the roster is harness-owned and refreshes with the gate in the same update)

rls-isolation.ref: RLS runner FAILS CLOSED without a database (CI=true)

unit.note: vitest exits non-zero on failing tests or coverage-threshold misses

diff-coverage.note: an uncovered new src file (absent from coverage-final.json) and a below-floor changed file both red the Stop chain naming file + metric; missing coverage-final.json fails closed

duplication.note: a token-clone (>=70 tokens / 6 lines) pasted across apps/*/src or packages/*/src reds naming both sites + a content fingerprint; the reviewed tools/duplication-allow.json mutes an accepted clone by fingerprint and malformed/absent entries fail closed; a pre-0.1.6 baseVersion downgrades a clone to a ramp NOTE (green) so existing copy-paste never ambushes an update, turn-fatal from 0.1.6
